title: Stuck Keypad or Control Board Failure
shortDescription: The control board detects a continuously pressed key or an internal logic failure.
fullDescription: Error F0 indicates the electronic oven control (EOC) has detected a stuck key on the touchpad membrane or an internal control board malfunction. The board continuously receives a signal as if a button is being pressed, or its internal logic test has failed. This prevents normal oven operation and may cause the oven to behave erratically.

causes.cause: Touchpad membrane has a stuck or shorted key
Electronic oven control board has failed
Ribbon cable between keypad and control board is damaged
Moisture or grease has infiltrated the touchpad membrane

symptoms: Oven will not respond to button presses
Oven may turn on or off by itself
Display flashes or shows random characters

fixSteps.title: Power reset the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for the oven for 5 minutes, then restore power. This can clear a temporary glitch in the control board.

fixSteps.title: Clean the touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: Gently clean the touchpad surface with a damp cloth. Do not use harsh chemicals. If moisture has gotten behind the pad, allow it to dry thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel or the front control panel cover. Check the flat ribbon cable connecting the touchpad to the control board. Reseat or replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Replace the touchpad or control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, replace the touchpad membrane first. If that does not resolve it, replace the electronic oven control board.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the touchpad does not resolve F0, a professional should diagnose whether the control board or wiring is at fault.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F0 error stuck key control board fix

faq.q: Is F0 dangerous?
faq.a: If the oven turns on by itself, it can be a safety hazard. Disconnect power until the repair is complete.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F0?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges can damage the control board's logic circuitry, causing F0. A surge protector on the circuit is recommended.

faq.q: Should I replace the touchpad or control board first?
faq.a: Start with the touchpad since it is cheaper. On some GE models the touchpad and control board are sold as a combined unit.

title: Electronic Oven Control Board Failure
shortDescription: The electronic oven control board has detected an internal failure.
fullDescription: Error F1 indicates a hardware failure within the electronic oven control (EOC) board itself. The board's internal self-test has detected a malfunction in its EEPROM, processor, or relay circuits. The oven cannot operate safely and will lock out all functions.

causes.cause: Electronic oven control board has failed internally
Power surge damaged the control board
Loose or corroded connection at the control board
Thermal damage from oven heat reaching the control board

symptoms: Oven beeps repeatedly and displays F1
Clock and timer functions may still work

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes and then restore power. If F1 returns immediately, the board has a permanent failure.

fixSteps.title: Check board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel to access the control board. Inspect all wire connectors for looseness, corrosion, or heat damage. Reseat each connector firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the board for visible damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burned components, cracked solder joints, or swollen capacitors on the control board. Any visible damage confirms the board needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the electronic oven control board
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove all wire connectors from the old board, noting their positions. Remove mounting screws, swap boards, reconnect all wires.

whenToCallPro: F1 almost always requires control board replacement. A professional can confirm the diagnosis and ensure the correct replacement board.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F1 error control board replacement

faq.q: Can F1 be fixed without replacing the board?
faq.a: Rarely. F1 indicates an internal board failure. Occasionally reseating connectors or clearing a power glitch can help, but most F1 codes require board replacement.

faq.q: How long do GE oven control boards last?
faq.a: Typically 10 to 15 years, but power surges can cause premature failure at any age.

faq.q: Can I repair the control board instead of replacing it?
faq.a: Some appliance repair services offer board-level repair for less cost. Search for appliance control board repair services in your area.

title: Oven Over-Temperature Condition
shortDescription: The oven temperature has exceeded the maximum safe limit.
fullDescription: Error F2 indicates the oven cavity temperature has exceeded the maximum safe threshold, typically around 590-615 degrees Fahrenheit during normal baking or 950 degrees during self-clean. The control board shuts down the heating elements to prevent damage or fire. This can occur due to a faulty temperature sensor, a stuck relay, or a shorted heating element.

causes.cause: Oven temperature sensor has failed or drifted out of calibration
Bake or broil relay on the control board is stuck closed
Heating element is partially shorted, drawing excessive current
Oven door seal is damaged, causing inaccurate temperature readings

symptoms: Smoke or burning smell from oven
Oven turns off and locks the door

fixSteps.title: Allow the oven to cool
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the oven and open the door carefully. Allow it to cool completely before performing any diagnostics. This may take 30 to 60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the sensor resistance at the connector behind the oven. At room temperature it should read approximately 1080-1100 ohms for an RTD sensor. A reading far from this indicates a failed sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the single mounting screw inside the oven cavity holding the sensor probe. Pull the old sensor out and feed the new one in. Reconnect at the back of the oven.

fixSteps.title: Check for a stuck relay
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is good but the oven continues to overheat, the bake or broil relay on the control board may be stuck closed. This requires control board replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the temperature sensor is good, a stuck relay on the control board requires professional replacement. Do not continue using an overheating oven.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F2 over temperature error fix

faq.q: Is F2 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. An overheating oven is a fire hazard. Do not use the oven until F2 is resolved.

faq.q: Why did F2 appear during self-clean?
faq.a: Self-clean reaches very high temperatures. If the sensor has drifted even slightly, it may trigger F2 during self-clean before it would during normal baking.

faq.q: Can I recalibrate the temperature sensor?
faq.a: The sensor itself cannot be recalibrated. Some GE models allow a temperature offset adjustment in the control settings, but this does not fix a failed sensor.

title: Oven Temperature Sensor Open or Shorted
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor circuit is reading as open or shorted.
fullDescription: Error F3 indicates the electronic oven control has detected that the oven temperature sensor (RTD probe) circuit is either open (infinite resistance) or shorted (near zero resistance). Without a valid temperature reading, the oven cannot regulate heating and will not operate.

causes.cause: Temperature sensor probe has failed open or shorted
Sensor wiring is broken or pinched
Sensor connector is disconnected or corroded
Control board sensor input circuit has failed

symptoms: Display shows dashes instead of temperature
Oven shuts off shortly after starting

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the rear of the oven. Locate the temperature sensor connector and ensure it is firmly plugged in. Look for corrosion or burned contacts.

fixSteps.title: Measure sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature, an RTD sensor should read 1080-1100 ohms. OL means open circuit, near zero means shorted.

fixSteps.title: Check the sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wires from the sensor connector back to where they enter the oven cavity. Look for damaged insulation, pinched wires, or burn marks.

fixSteps.title: Replace the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw inside the oven cavity. Pull the old sensor out through the back wall. Install the new sensor and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the new sensor does not clear F3, the control board sensor input may have failed and requires professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F3 temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between F2 and F3?
faq.a: F2 means the oven is actually overheating. F3 means the sensor circuit itself is faulty, preventing the oven from reading temperature at all.

faq.q: Is the temperature sensor easy to replace?
faq.a: Yes. It is one of the easiest oven repairs. One screw inside the oven, pull out the old sensor, feed in the new one, and reconnect the plug.

faq.q: Can I use my oven with F3?
faq.a: No. Without a functioning temperature sensor, the oven cannot regulate temperature and could overheat.

title: Oven Sensor Shorted
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor is reading a shorted condition.
fullDescription: Error F4 specifically indicates the oven temperature sensor circuit has a short, meaning the resistance reading is abnormally low. The control board interprets this as an impossibly high temperature and shuts down for safety. This is distinct from F3 which covers both open and shorted conditions on some models.

causes.cause: Temperature sensor has internally shorted
Sensor wiring is pinched, causing a short between conductors
Moisture inside the sensor connector causing a short
Control board analog input is damaged

symptoms: Temperature reading jumps to maximum immediately
Error appears at power up or shortly after starting

fixSteps.title: Disconnect and test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the rear panel and disconnect the temperature sensor. Measure resistance. A shorted sensor will read near 0 ohms instead of 1080-1100 ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Check the sensor connector for moisture or condensation. Dry thoroughly with compressed air. Moisture can create a low-resistance path between the sensor leads.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring for shorts
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the sensor wiring for areas where insulation has been damaged and bare wires are touching each other or the oven chassis.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw inside the oven. Pull out the old sensor, install the new one, and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If a new sensor does not clear F4, the control board needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F4 sensor shorted error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between F3 and F4?
faq.a: F3 typically indicates an open sensor circuit, while F4 indicates a shorted sensor circuit. Both require sensor replacement in most cases.

faq.q: Can steam from cooking cause F4?
faq.a: Yes. Steam can condense in the sensor connector, temporarily creating a short. Drying the connector may resolve F4 without replacing the sensor.

faq.q: Is the same sensor used for all GE ovens?
faq.a: No. While many GE models share the same sensor part number, always verify the correct part for your specific model.

title: Door Latch Switch Failure
shortDescription: The oven door latch switch is not operating correctly during self-clean.
fullDescription: Error F5 indicates the oven door latch mechanism or its associated switches are not functioning properly. During self-clean mode, the door must be securely latched and locked. If the control board cannot confirm the door is locked, or if the latch motor cannot engage, F5 is displayed and self-clean will not start or will abort mid-cycle.

causes.cause: Door latch motor or solenoid has failed
Door latch switch is broken or misaligned
Latch mechanism is jammed with food debris
Wiring to the latch assembly is damaged

symptoms: Door latch makes clicking or grinding noise
Door will not lock or unlock
Oven may be locked after a failed self-clean

fixSteps.title: Power reset to unlock door
fixSteps.instruction: If the door is locked, turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. When power returns, the latch motor should retract and unlock the door.

fixSteps.title: Clean the latch mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: With the door open, locate the latch at the top of the door opening. Clean any food residue or grease that may be jamming the mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Test the latch switch
fixSteps.instruction: Access the latch assembly behind the control panel. Test the latch switches for continuity in the locked and unlocked positions.

fixSteps.title: Replace the latch assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the control panel or rear access panel. Unscrew the latch assembly, disconnect wiring, and install the new assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the door is stuck locked and cannot be freed with a power reset, call a professional to avoid damaging the door mechanism.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F5 door latch error self clean fix

faq.q: My oven door is stuck locked after F5. What do I do?
faq.a: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. When power is restored, the latch motor should retract. If it does not, the latch assembly needs replacement.

faq.q: Can I still bake with F5?
faq.a: Often yes. F5 typically only prevents self-clean mode. Normal baking that does not require the door latch may still work.

faq.q: How often should I run self-clean?
faq.a: GE recommends no more than once per month. Frequent self-clean cycles put extra stress on the latch mechanism and door seal.

title: Stuck Function Key
shortDescription: A specific function key on the touchpad is stuck or shorted.
fullDescription: Error F7 indicates a specific function button on the oven touchpad is stuck in a pressed state. Unlike F0 which is a general keypad or control board issue, F7 points to a particular key. The control board continuously receives input from one button, preventing normal operation.

causes.cause: A specific touchpad button is physically stuck
Touchpad membrane has a short at one key location
Moisture or food residue is bridging contacts under a key
Ribbon cable to the touchpad has a partial short

symptoms: A specific button appears to be pressed without touching it
Oven will not accept any input

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. If F7 returns immediately on power-up, the keypad membrane is likely faulty.

fixSteps.title: Clean the touchpad area
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the touchpad with a slightly damp soft cloth. Pay attention to the areas around each button. Ensure no food residue is pressing on any key.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect the touchpad ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Access the control board and disconnect the ribbon cable from the touchpad. If the error clears with the touchpad disconnected, the touchpad is the problem.

fixSteps.title: Replace the touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: Order the touchpad membrane for your model. Remove the old membrane and carefully apply the new one, reconnecting the ribbon cable.

whenToCallPro: If both the touchpad and control board have been replaced and F7 persists, a professional should inspect the wiring.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F7 stuck key error touchpad fix

faq.q: Is F7 the same as F0?
faq.a: They are similar. F0 is a general keypad or control board logic error. F7 specifically identifies that a particular function key is stuck.

faq.q: Can I temporarily silence the F7 alarm?
faq.a: Turning off the circuit breaker is the only way to silence the alarm. Do not just turn off the oven control as the alarm may continue.

faq.q: Do aftermarket touchpads work for GE ovens?
faq.a: Some aftermarket touchpads are available at lower cost. Ensure the part number is compatible with your specific model.

title: Configuration or EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The control board EEPROM data is corrupted or the board is misconfigured.
fullDescription: Error F8 indicates the electronic oven control board has detected an error in its EEPROM (configuration memory). The EEPROM stores model-specific calibration data, temperature offsets, and operational parameters. If this data is corrupted, the board cannot operate the oven correctly.

causes.cause: EEPROM data on the control board is corrupted
Power surge or outage during EEPROM write operation
Control board is defective from the factory
Wrong control board installed for this model

symptoms: Oven will not operate at all
Cannot clear error with power reset
All oven functions are locked out

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes and restore power. Sometimes an extended power-off can allow the board to reinitialize its EEPROM.

fixSteps.title: Verify the correct board is installed
fixSteps.instruction: Check the part number on the existing control board against the correct part number for your oven model. If a previous repair installed the wrong board, this can cause F8.

fixSteps.title: Check board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all connectors on the control board. A loose connection can occasionally cause communication errors that corrupt EEPROM data.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: F8 typically requires control board replacement as the EEPROM cannot be reprogrammed in the field. Disconnect power, swap the board, and reconnect all wiring.

whenToCallPro: F8 almost always requires board replacement. A professional can ensure the correct replacement board with proper EEPROM programming.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F8 EEPROM configuration error fix

faq.q: What is EEPROM?
faq.a: EEPROM (Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory) is a small memory chip on the control board that stores calibration data and settings specific to your oven model.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F8?
faq.a: Yes. If a power surge or outage occurs while the board is writing to EEPROM, the data can become corrupted.

faq.q: Is F8 covered by warranty?
faq.a: If the oven is within the manufacturer warranty period, F8 due to a defective board should be covered. Check your warranty terms.

title: Door Lock Failure
shortDescription: The oven door lock mechanism has failed to engage or disengage properly.
fullDescription: Error F9 indicates the oven door lock mechanism cannot complete its locking or unlocking cycle within the expected time. The lock motor runs but the latch switch does not change state, indicating a mechanical jam, motor failure, or switch malfunction. This is critical during self-clean mode where the door must be locked.

causes.cause: Door lock motor has failed
Latch mechanism is physically jammed
Door lock switch has failed
Wiring to the lock assembly is broken

symptoms: Door will not lock for self-clean
Door is stuck in the locked position
Clicking sound from the lock motor area

fixSteps.title: Power reset to release the lock
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. The lock motor should reset when power returns. If the door unlocks, the jam may have been temporary.

fixSteps.title: Manually release the lock
fixSteps.instruction: If the door remains locked, remove the rear access panel. Locate the lock motor assembly and look for a manual release lever or tab.

fixSteps.title: Inspect and clean the latch
fixSteps.instruction: With the door open, clean the latch mechanism at the top of the door opening. Remove any food debris or carbon buildup from self-clean cycles.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the control panel or rear panel to access the lock assembly. Disconnect wiring, remove mounting screws, and install the new assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the door is stuck locked and cannot be freed manually, a professional can safely release it without damaging the door or lock mechanism.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F9 door lock error fix

faq.q: Can I force the oven door open if it is locked?
faq.a: Do not force it. This can damage the latch mechanism and the door itself. Use the power reset method or manual release lever instead.

faq.q: Is F9 the same as F5?
faq.a: They are related but different. F5 indicates the latch switch is not registering correctly. F9 means the lock motor could not complete its cycle within the expected time.

faq.q: Does F9 affect normal baking?
faq.a: F9 usually only affects self-clean mode. Normal baking does not require the door lock and should still work.

title: Stuck Touch Key Detected
shortDescription: The control board detects a continuously activated touch key on the glass panel.
fullDescription: Error F0E0 appears on newer GE ovens with glass touch controls. It indicates a touch key is registering as continuously pressed. This is common on models with capacitive touch panels where moisture, food residue, or a panel defect can cause false touches.

causes.cause: Moisture or food residue on the glass touch panel
Glass touch panel has a defective capacitive sensor
Control board touch input circuit has failed
Ribbon cable between panel and board is damaged

symptoms: Touch panel does not respond to inputs
Error may come and go with humidity changes

fixSteps.title: Clean the touch panel
fixSteps.instruction: Thoroughly clean the glass touch panel with a non-abrasive glass cleaner. Remove all food residue, grease, and moisture. Dry completely.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. If the error was caused by a temporary moisture issue, it may not return.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the control panel cover and check the ribbon cable connecting the glass touch panel to the control board. Look for damage, bends, or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the glass touch panel
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after cleaning and reseating cables, the touch panel assembly needs replacement. Disconnect power, remove the old panel, and install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve the issue and you are unsure whether the touch panel or control board is at fault.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F0E0 touch key error glass panel fix

faq.q: What GE models use F0E0 codes?
faq.a: The F-E combined codes are used on GE Profile and Cafe series ovens with glass touch control panels.

faq.q: Can humidity cause F0E0?
faq.a: Yes. High humidity or steam from cooking can cause the capacitive touch panel to register false touches, triggering F0E0.

faq.q: Is F0E0 covered under warranty?
faq.a: If the touch panel is defective within the warranty period, it should be covered. Damage from improper cleaning or external moisture may not be covered.

title: Control Board Watchdog Circuit Tripped
shortDescription: The control board's internal watchdog timer has detected a processing error.
fullDescription: Error F1E0 indicates the control board's watchdog circuit has tripped. The watchdog timer is a safety feature that monitors the main processor. If the processor hangs or enters an invalid state, the watchdog resets the board and displays F1E0. Repeated occurrences indicate a board failure.

causes.cause: Control board processor has intermittent failure
Power supply voltage to the board is unstable
Electrical noise from a faulty component is interfering with the processor
Temporary software glitch from power fluctuation

symptoms: Display may flash or go blank momentarily

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. A single F1E0 occurrence may be a temporary glitch. If it does not return, monitor for recurrence.

fixSteps.title: Check the power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the oven is receiving proper voltage at the outlet. A 240V oven should read 240V (+/- 10V) between the hot legs.

fixSteps.title: Inspect board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Check all wire connectors on the control board for looseness. A poor ground connection can cause electrical noise that disrupts the processor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If F1E0 recurs frequently, the control board needs replacement. Disconnect power, swap boards, reconnect all wiring.

whenToCallPro: If F1E0 occurs repeatedly after power cycling, the board is failing and needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F1E0 watchdog error control board fix

faq.q: Is a single F1E0 occurrence serious?
faq.a: Not necessarily. A one-time F1E0 can be caused by a power fluctuation. If it clears after a power cycle and does not return, monitor the oven but it is likely fine.

faq.q: What is a watchdog timer?
faq.a: A watchdog timer is a hardware safety circuit that monitors the processor. If the processor stops responding within a set time, the watchdog resets the system to prevent unsafe operation.

faq.q: Can a dedicated circuit for the oven prevent F1E0?
faq.a: A dedicated circuit with proper voltage and a surge protector can reduce the chance of power-related F1E0 errors.

title: Control Board EEPROM Communication Error
shortDescription: The control board cannot read or write to its internal EEPROM memory.
fullDescription: Error F1E1 on newer GE ovens indicates the control board's main processor cannot communicate with its EEPROM memory chip. The EEPROM stores calibration data, user settings, and operational parameters. If the processor cannot access this data, the oven cannot function.

causes.cause: EEPROM chip on the control board has failed
Solder joint failure between EEPROM and the circuit board
Power surge corrupted the EEPROM communication bus
Temporary glitch from power interruption

symptoms: Settings are lost after each power cycle

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 10 minutes. An extended power-off can sometimes allow the EEPROM to recover.

fixSteps.title: Check for power supply issues
fixSteps.instruction: Verify stable voltage at the oven outlet. Fluctuating voltage can cause EEPROM read/write failures.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Check for visible damage, burned components, or cracked solder joints on the control board, especially around the small EEPROM chip.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: F1E1 requires board replacement. Disconnect power, document wiring positions, swap boards, and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: F1E1 almost always requires board replacement. A professional ensures correct board selection and programming.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F1E1 EEPROM error control board replacement

faq.q: Can the EEPROM be reprogrammed?
faq.a: Not in the field. The EEPROM is soldered to the control board and requires factory programming. Board replacement is the solution.

faq.q: Is F1E1 common on GE Profile ovens?
faq.a: F1E1 is not extremely common but occurs with enough frequency on Profile and Cafe models to be well documented by technicians.

faq.q: Will a surge protector prevent F1E1?
faq.a: A surge protector can help prevent power surge damage that may cause EEPROM failure, but it cannot prevent all causes of F1E1.

title: Oven Cavity Over-Temperature (Upper Oven)
shortDescription: The upper oven cavity has exceeded the maximum safe temperature.
fullDescription: Error F2E0 on dual-oven GE models indicates the upper oven cavity has exceeded its maximum safe temperature threshold. The control shuts down the upper heating elements. This is the two-digit equivalent of F2 on newer control systems that differentiate between upper and lower ovens.

causes.cause: Upper oven temperature sensor has failed or drifted
Bake relay for the upper oven is stuck closed
Upper oven heating element is partially shorted
Upper oven door gasket is damaged or missing

symptoms: Smoke or burning smell from upper oven
Food burns in the upper oven

fixSteps.title: Let the oven cool
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the oven and open the door carefully. Allow the upper oven to cool completely for 30-60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test the upper oven sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate and disconnect the upper oven temperature sensor. Measure resistance: should be 1080-1100 ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace the upper oven sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw inside the upper oven. Pull the old sensor out and install the replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck relay
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is good, test whether the bake element receives power even when off. A stuck relay means the control board needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests good, the stuck relay requires board replacement by a qualified technician.
videoSearchQuery: GE double oven F2E0 over temperature upper oven fix

faq.q: What does E0 mean in the F2E0 code?
faq.a: E0 designates the upper oven in dual-oven GE models. E1 would refer to the lower oven.

faq.q: Can F2E0 cause a fire?
faq.a: An oven that continues to heat unchecked can be a fire hazard. Disconnect power immediately if the oven appears to overheat.

faq.q: Do both ovens share the same control board?
faq.a: Yes. In most GE double ovens, a single control board manages both the upper and lower ovens.

title: Upper Oven Temperature Sensor Open
shortDescription: The upper oven temperature sensor circuit reads as an open circuit.
fullDescription: Error F3E0 on dual-oven GE models indicates the upper oven temperature sensor has an open circuit. The control board receives no temperature reading from the upper oven and cannot regulate heating. The upper oven will not operate until the sensor is replaced.

causes.cause: Upper oven temperature sensor has failed open
Sensor connector is disconnected or corroded
Sensor wiring is broken, especially near the oven cavity
Control board sensor input has failed

symptoms: Upper oven display shows dashes for temperature
Lower oven may still function normally
Error appears when upper oven is selected

fixSteps.title: Check the sensor connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the rear of the oven and locate the upper oven sensor connector. Ensure it is firmly seated and not corroded.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. Should read 1080-1100 ohms at room temperature. OL indicates an open sensor.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the sensor wires from the connector to the oven cavity entry point. Look for breaks, especially where heat exposure is greatest.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the single mounting screw inside the upper oven cavity. Pull out the old sensor and feed in the new one. Reconnect at the back.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not clear F3E0, the control board input circuit may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE double oven F3E0 upper oven sensor open fix

faq.q: Does F3E0 affect the lower oven?
faq.a: No. F3E0 only affects the upper oven. The lower oven has its own sensor and should continue to function normally.

faq.q: Are the upper and lower oven sensors the same part?
faq.a: On most GE double ovens, the upper and lower sensors are the same part number, but always verify for your specific model.

faq.q: Can I use the lower oven while F3E0 is active?
faq.a: Yes, the lower oven operates independently and should work normally.

title: Lower Oven Temperature Sensor Open
shortDescription: The lower oven temperature sensor circuit reads as an open circuit.
fullDescription: Error F3E1 on dual-oven GE models indicates the lower oven temperature sensor has an open circuit. The control board cannot read temperature from the lower oven. The lower oven will not operate. The upper oven is unaffected.

causes.cause: Lower oven temperature sensor has failed open
Sensor connector for the lower oven is disconnected
Sensor wiring has broken due to heat exposure
Control board lower sensor input has failed

symptoms: Error appears when lower oven is selected

fixSteps.title: Check the lower sensor connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the rear of the oven and locate the lower oven sensor connector. Reseat it firmly.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. Should be 1080-1100 ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check sensor wiring for breaks or heat damage along the full run from connector to oven cavity.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw inside the lower oven cavity, pull out the old sensor, install the replacement, and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the new sensor does not clear F3E1, the control board needs professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: GE double oven F3E1 lower oven sensor fix

faq.q: Can I still use the upper oven?
faq.a: Yes, the upper oven operates on a separate sensor and should work normally while F3E1 is active on the lower oven.

faq.q: Is the lower sensor harder to access?
faq.a: The lower sensor is usually accessed the same way as the upper. It mounts inside the lower oven cavity with a single screw.

faq.q: How often do oven sensors fail?
faq.a: Oven temperature sensors typically last 5-15 years. Frequent self-clean cycles can shorten sensor lifespan due to extreme heat exposure.

title: Upper Oven Door Latch Error
shortDescription: The upper oven door latch failed to lock or unlock during self-clean.
fullDescription: Error F5E0 on dual-oven GE models indicates the upper oven door lock mechanism failed to complete its cycle. The lock motor ran but the latch switch did not confirm the expected locked or unlocked state. Self-clean cannot proceed without a confirmed door lock.

causes.cause: Upper oven door latch motor has failed
Latch mechanism is jammed with debris
Latch switch is broken or misaligned
Wiring to the upper latch assembly is damaged

symptoms: Upper oven self-clean will not start
Upper oven door will not lock or unlock
Clicking noise from the upper oven latch area
Upper oven door stuck in locked position

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes to reset the latch motor and unlock the door if stuck.

fixSteps.title: Clean the latch mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: With the upper oven door open, clean the latch at the top of the door opening. Remove food debris and grease buildup.

fixSteps.title: Test the latch switches
fixSteps.instruction: Access the latch assembly and test switch continuity in both locked and unlocked positions.

fixSteps.title: Replace the upper latch assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the control panel to access the upper latch. Disconnect wiring, remove screws, and install new assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the door is stuck locked and a power reset does not free it.
videoSearchQuery: GE double oven F5E0 upper door latch error fix

faq.q: Can I still bake in the upper oven with F5E0?
faq.a: Usually yes. F5E0 typically only prevents self-clean mode. Normal baking does not require the door lock.

faq.q: What does E0 mean in the error code?
faq.a: E0 refers to the upper oven cavity in GE dual-oven models.

faq.q: How do I manually unlock the upper oven door?
faq.a: After a power reset, the latch motor should retract. If not, look for a manual release tab accessible from behind the control panel.

title: Lower Oven Door Latch Error
shortDescription: The lower oven door latch failed to lock or unlock during self-clean.
fullDescription: Error F5E1 on dual-oven GE models indicates the lower oven door lock mechanism could not complete its locking or unlocking cycle. The latch motor ran but the switch did not confirm the expected state. This prevents self-clean from operating on the lower oven.

causes.cause: Lower oven door latch motor has failed
Lower oven latch mechanism is physically jammed
Latch switch is defective
Wiring to lower latch is damaged

symptoms: Lower oven self-clean will not start
Lower oven door stuck locked or will not lock
Clicking from the lower oven latch area

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. This should reset the lock motor and free a stuck door.

fixSteps.title: Clean the latch mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Open the lower oven door and clean the latch at the top of the door opening, removing debris and carbon buildup.

fixSteps.title: Test latch switches
fixSteps.instruction: Access the lower latch assembly and test switch continuity in locked and unlocked states.

fixSteps.title: Replace the lower latch assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the lower latch assembly, disconnect wiring, remove mounting screws, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the door remains stuck locked after all steps.
videoSearchQuery: GE double oven F5E1 lower door latch error fix

faq.q: Can I use the lower oven for baking?
faq.a: Yes, normal baking does not require the door lock and should work despite F5E1.

faq.q: Is the lower oven latch the same part as the upper?
faq.a: On many GE double ovens they share the same part number, but always verify for your specific model.

faq.q: How do I prevent latch jams?
faq.a: Wipe down the latch area regularly to prevent food and grease buildup. Avoid running self-clean more than once per month.

title: Lower Oven Over-Temperature Condition
shortDescription: The lower oven cavity has exceeded its maximum safe temperature.
fullDescription: Error F2E1 on dual-oven GE models indicates the lower oven cavity has exceeded the maximum safe temperature limit. The control board shuts down the lower oven heating elements. This is the lower oven counterpart to F2E0.

causes.cause: Lower oven temperature sensor has failed or drifted
Bake relay for the lower oven is stuck closed
Lower oven bake element is partially shorted
Lower oven door gasket is damaged

fixSteps.title: Cool the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the oven and open the lower door carefully. Allow it to cool for 30-60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test lower oven sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the lower oven sensor and measure resistance. Should be 1080-1100 ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace lower oven sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw inside the lower oven, pull out the old sensor, and install the new one.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck relay
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests good, check whether the lower bake element receives power even when turned off. A stuck relay requires board replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor is fine, a stuck relay on the board needs professional replacement. Do not use an overheating oven.
videoSearchQuery: GE double oven F2E1 lower oven over temperature fix

faq.q: Is F2E1 a fire risk?
faq.a: Yes. An oven that overheats uncontrollably is a fire hazard. Disconnect power immediately and do not use until repaired.

faq.q: Can I still use the upper oven?
faq.a: Yes, the upper oven is independent and should work normally.

faq.q: Can a dirty oven cause F2E1?
faq.a: Not directly, but excessive grease buildup can cause smoke and flare-ups that raise temperature beyond the threshold.

title: Control Board Relay Failure
shortDescription: A heating relay on the control board is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error F6 indicates that one of the heating element relays on the electronic oven control board has failed. The relay may be stuck open (element cannot turn on) or stuck closed (element cannot turn off). This is a control board level failure that typically requires board replacement.

causes.cause: Bake or broil relay on the control board has failed
Heavy use has worn out the relay contacts
Power surge damaged the relay circuit
Shorted heating element caused excessive relay current

symptoms: Oven will not heat at all (stuck open relay)
Oven will not stop heating (stuck closed relay)
Only bake or only broil works, not both
Clicking sound from the control board area

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. If F6 clears and does not return, monitor the oven for recurrence.

fixSteps.title: Check the heating elements
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect bake and broil elements for visible damage, blistering, or breaks. A shorted element can damage the relay. Replace any damaged element before replacing the board.

fixSteps.title: Test element resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect each element and measure resistance. A bake element should read 20-50 ohms, broil element 15-40 ohms. Very low readings indicate a short.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If elements are good, the relay on the board has failed. Disconnect power, swap the control board, and reconnect all wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the oven will not stop heating (stuck closed relay), disconnect power immediately and call a professional. This is a safety hazard.
videoSearchQuery: GE oven F6 relay failure control board fix

faq.q: Is a stuck closed relay dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. A relay stuck closed means the heating element runs continuously and cannot be turned off by the control. Disconnect power immediately.

faq.q: Can I replace just the relay on the board?
faq.a: Technically yes, if you have soldering skills. However, most repair professionals replace the entire board for reliability.

faq.q: Should I check elements before replacing the board?
faq.a: Yes. A shorted element can damage a new control board. Always verify element resistance before installing a replacement board.

title: Upper Oven Sensor Shorted (Double Oven)
shortDescription: The upper oven temperature sensor has a short circuit on dual-oven GE models.
fullDescription: Error F4E0 on dual-oven GE models indicates the upper oven temperature sensor has a short circuit. The control board reads near-zero resistance and interprets it as extreme heat. The upper oven will not operate.

causes.cause: Upper oven sensor has internally shorted
Sensor wiring is pinched creating a short
Moisture in the upper sensor connector
Control board upper sensor input has failed

symptoms: Upper oven temperature jumps to max reading
Error appears when upper oven is selected

fixSteps.title: Test the upper sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the upper oven sensor and measure resistance. Should be 1080-1100 ohms at room temp. Near zero indicates a short.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Dry the sensor connector with compressed air. Steam from cooking can short the leads.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check sensor wires for damaged insulation where bare wires may be touching.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screw inside upper oven, pull out old sensor, install new one.

whenToCallPro: If a new sensor does not clear F4E0, the control board needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE double oven F4E0 upper sensor shorted fix

faq.q: Can moisture from cooking cause F4E0?
faq.a: Yes. Steam can condense in the sensor connector, creating a low-resistance path that appears as a short.

faq.q: Can I use the lower oven?
faq.a: Yes, the lower oven operates independently.

faq.q: Is F4E0 the same fix as F3E0?
faq.a: Same sensor and procedure. F3E0 is open circuit, F4E0 is shorted circuit.
